title: Treatment of inflammatory disease or condition
abstract: The present invention provides a method for treatment of an inflammatory disease or condition in a subject using interleukin 2 (IL-2) in combination with regulatory T cells (Treg). The Tregs may be autologous and/or treated with IL-2 ex vivo.
claims: Claims 1 . Interleukin 2 (IL-2) in combination with regulatory T cells (Treg) for use in the treatment of an inflammatory disease or condition in a subject. 2. IL-2 in combination with Treg for use according to claim 1 , wherein the Treg are autologous. 3. IL-2 in combination with Treg for use according to claim 1 or claim 2, wherein the inflammatory disease or condition is inflammation of a solid organ. 4. IL-2 in combination with Treg for use according to claim 3, wherein the inflammatory disease or condition is an autoimmune liver disease or an inflammatory liver disease. 5. IL-2 in combination with Treg for use according to claim 4, wherein the autoimmune liver disease is autoimmune hepatitis, autoimmune hepatobiliary disease (AIHBD), primary sclerosing cholangitis, primary biliary cirrhosis or overlap syndrome. 6. IL-2 in combination with Treg for use according to any one of claims 1 to 3, wherein the inflammatory disease or condition is post-transplant rejection. 7. IL-2 in combination with Treg for use according to any preceding claim, wherein the IL-2 is administered at a dose sufficient to achieve a local concentration of from 0.1 to 100 lU/ml. 8. IL-2 in combination with Treg for use according to any preceding claim, wherein the Treg are administered at a dose of from 1 million to 4 million cells per kg body weight. 9. IL-2 in combination with Treg for use according to any preceding claim, wherein the subject to be treated displays an increased level of CXCR3 ligand expression, or a level of CXCR3 ligand which is above a predetermined threshold. 10. Treg for use in the treatment of an inflammatory disease or condition, wherein said use comprises the administration of Treg to a subject, and wherein said Treg have been activated by ex vivo treatment with IL-2 prior to administration. 1 1 . IL-2 for use in the treatment of an inflammatory disease or condition of a solid organ. 12. A pharmaceutical formulation comprising Treg, IL-2, and a pharmaceutically acceptable carrier, diluent or excipient. 13. A pharmaceutical formulation comprising Treg and a pharmaceutically acceptable carrier, diluent or excipient, wherein said Treg have been activated by ex vivo treatment with IL-2. 14. A method for identifying a subject having an inflammatory disease or condition of the liver likely to respond to Treg and/or IL-2 therapy, said method comprising determining a level of CXCR3 ligand expression in a sample obtained from the subject. 15. The method according to claim 14, comprising determining the level of expression of at least one of CXCL9 (MIG), CXCL10 (IP-10) and CXCL1 1 (l-TAC). 16. The method according to claim 14 or 15, wherein a subject is identified as likely to respond to Treg and/or IL-2 therapy if the level of CXCR3 ligand expression is above a predetermined threshold or if the level of CXCR3 ligand expression is increased relative to a reference level. 17. A method of treating an inflammatory disease or condition in a subject, the method comprising administration of Treg and IL-2 to said subject. 18. A method of treating inflammation of a solid organ, in a subject, the method comprising administration of IL-2 to said subject. 19. A method of treating an inflammatory disease or condition in a subject, the method comprising administration of Treg to said subject, wherein said Treg have been activated by ex vivo treatment with IL-2. 20. A method of treating an inflammatory disease or condition of the liver in a subject, the method comprising: - determining a level of CXCR3 ligand expression in a sample obtained from the subject; and - if the level of CXCR3 ligand expression is increased relative to a control sample, or if the level of CXCR3 ligand expression is above a predetermined threshold, administering to said subject: (i) Treg and IL-2; (ii) IL-2; or (iii) Treg which have been activated ex vivo by treatment with IL-2 prior to administration. 21 . A method of treating an inflammatory disease or condition of the liver in a subject, the method comprising administering: (i) Treg and IL-2; (ii) IL-2; or (iii) Treg which have been activated ex vivo by treatment with IL-2 prior to administration to a subject determined as having a level of CXCR3 ligand expression in a sample obtained from said subject which is increased relative to a control sample or which is above a predetermined threshold.
